body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full
driver_capability: ready

(Reassessment of opencode CLI v1.14.50 against the current daemon and the current `drive-opencode-interactive.sh`. The prior verdict was `driver_capability: gap:slash-command`; that gap has since been closed — see "Driver" below. This flips the cell from driver-gap to record.)

## Reasoning

### Agent supports it (agent_supports: yes)

opencode v1.14.50 ships in-session compaction in two forms:

- **Manual** — a compaction command registered as `command.session.compact` (found verbatim in the v1.14.50 binary strings), with the `/summarize` slash alias also present as a literal, and a `session_compact` keybind (default `<leader>c`) documented on the official keybinds page. So the user can run one normal turn to populate context, then trigger compaction in place, then continue a second turn on the same session — exactly the `context_curation` primitive the scenario exercises.
- **Automatic** — on token overflow, opencode's `isOverflow` check (model context limit minus a reserved buffer) fires after an LLM step and the session loop inserts a `CompactionPart` into a new user message to queue summarization (`experimental.compaction.autocontinue` / `"compaction": { "auto": true }` config keys are present in the binary). This is the auto path the scenario lists as a co-trigger.

Either way compaction is an in-place LLM summarization round on the SAME session — no new session is minted (cf. the session-resume cell, which IS a fresh-transcript boundary; compaction is not).

### Daemon observes it (daemon_capability: full)

opencode is the lone `ProcessOwnedStore` adapter (`core/adapters/inbound/agents/opencode/agent.go`): the daemon reads session state straight from the SQLite store via the dedicated watcher (`watcher.go`) rather than tailing a transcript. The watcher subscribes to `opencode.db-wal` writes and, on each write, queries the `part` table for new/updated rows in known sessions and emits `EventActivity`; `isTerminalPart` recognizes a `step-finish` part and the parser maps `reason="stop"` to `turn_done` (`parser.go` `parseStepFinish`). A compaction round runs a summarization LLM step that lands a `step-finish` part on the existing session, so it surfaces to irrlicht as an ordinary `ready → working → ready` arc on that session — precisely the User-observable signal the scenario asserts (`ready → working` at compaction start; stays `working`; `working → ready` when done; no further transitions). The `CompactionPart` itself is an unrecognized part type, and the parser's `default` arm (`parser.go` lines 81-85) sets `Skip = true`, so it is harmless — it neither breaks the scan nor adds a spurious transition. The daemon cannot LABEL the turn as compaction (irrlicht's `CompactionState` domain field has no production writer), but the scenario asserts only the lifecycle arc, which IS observable. The daemon is not the blocker.

### Driver can drive it (driver_capability: ready) — THE CHANGE THIS REASSESSMENT MAKES

The prior assessment set `driver_capability: gap:slash-command` because `drive-opencode-interactive.sh` then handled only `send|wait_turn|sleep` over headless `opencode run`, and headless `opencode run` stores a `/summarize` as literal user text rather than executing it. That unblock action — "add a live-REPL/slash primitive to the opencode driver (tmux send-keys, like claudecode/codex/pi)" — has been implemented. The current driver has a `run_live()` path (lines 99-294) that launches the opencode TUI under tmux, waits for the "Ask anything" input affordance, and drives it with `tmux send-keys`. It implements a `slash` step type (lines 205-210: `tmux send-keys -l -- "$s"` then Enter) and a `reset_session` step, and the top-level router (lines 354-357) sends any script containing a `slash` (or `reset_session`) step to that live-TUI path. A recipe for this cell can therefore: `send` a priming turn (live path's `oc_send` + `oc_wait_turn` polling `step-finish` totals), then issue a `slash` step delivering `/summarize` (or the leader keybind via a `keys`-style send) inside the running REPL, then `send` a second turn — all on one session. The driver now has every step type the recipe needs; the gap is closed.

## What changed vs. the prior verdict

- agent_supports: yes → yes (unchanged; v1.14.50 confirmed `/summarize` + `command.session.compact` + `<leader>c` keybind + auto-compaction).
- daemon_capability: full → full (unchanged; re-grounded in watcher.go + parser.go for v1.14.50).
- driver_capability: gap:slash-command → **ready** (the live-TUI tmux `slash`/`reset_session` path now exists in drive-opencode-interactive.sh).

Route moves from `driver-gap` to `record`. The committed `expected.jsonl` already documents the target arc.

## Confidence

0.82: the driver's live-TUI `slash` primitive is read directly from the current driver source (router at lines 354-357, `slash` case at 205-210); daemon observability is grounded in watcher.go/parser.go; v1.14.50 ships `/summarize` + `command.session.compact` per the binary strings and the keybinds doc. Not higher only because the end-to-end live `/summarize` round has not yet been recorded against the small local model, and the exact in-TUI trigger (slash vs leader keybind) the recipe will fire is a recipe-stage detail — both forms are drivable by the live path.
caveats: irrlicht's CompactionState domain field is dead code (no production writer) — the daemon cannot distinguish a compaction turn from a normal long turn. The scenario asserts only the visible state arc, which IS observable; the compaction-vs-normal-turn label is not.

Manual compaction may surface as the `/summarize` slash command, the `command.session.compact` palette entry, or the `<leader>c` (session_compact) keybind. The driver's live-TUI path can deliver a `/summarize` slash via send-keys; if the running v1.14.50 build maps manual compaction only to the leader keybind, the recipe must send that key chord instead (still within the existing live-TUI send-keys primitive — not a new gap).

headless `opencode run` still cannot deliver in-TUI compaction (it stores slash text literally), so the recipe MUST carry a `slash` (or `reset_session`) step to force the driver onto the live-TUI tmux path; a send-only recipe would not trigger compaction.

sources.note: run_live() (lines 99-294) drives the opencode TUI under tmux; implements a `slash` step (lines 205-210: tmux send-keys -l + Enter) and reset_session; router (lines 354-357) sends any slash/reset_session script to the live path. This is the live-REPL/slash primitive whose absence drove the prior gap:slash-command verdict — it now exists, so driver_capability: ready.

lone ProcessOwnedStore adapter; watcher subscribes to opencode.db-wal and emits EventActivity from new `part` rows; isTerminalPart recognizes step-finish. A compaction summarization step lands a step-finish on the SAME session → ready→working→ready arc observed.

parseStepFinish maps reason=stop → turn_done (working→ready); the default arm (lines 81-85) Skips unrecognized part types, so a CompactionPart is harmless and adds no spurious transition — daemon_capability: full.

binary contains `command.session.compact`, the `/summarize` literal, `experimental.compaction.autocontinue`, `"compaction": { "auto": true`, and compaction-part DOM slots — manual + auto compaction both shipped in v1.14.50.
session_compact keybind (default <leader>c) — official confirmation manual compaction is a triggerable in-TUI command, deliverable via the driver's live-TUI send-keys path.

auto-compaction via isOverflow (context limit minus reserved buffer); inserts a CompactionPart into a new user message to queue summarization — confirms the auto path and the compaction-part storage shape the parser Skips.
